eval_prompt: SSH partial failure diagnostic: heavy-2 and heavy-3 failing, heavy-1 works. Diagnose SSH keys, known_hosts, network, aether config, cluster. Fix if possible.

criteria.description: Must test SSH connections to heavy-1/2/3 separately and report per-node results

output_a.notes: Tests all 8 nodes individually via hostname and IP. Clear per-node table with differentiated results.

output_b.notes: Tests all 3 heavy nodes via hostname and IP. Also checks remote auth logs on heavy-2/3 specifically.

criteria.description: Must attempt fixes after finding issues and verify success

output_a.notes: Identified SSH config gap (Host pattern not matching IP addresses). Applied fix: added IP wildcard patterns and ConnectTimeout to ~/.ssh/config. Shows before/after diff. Root cause directly explains the reported failure scenario.

output_b.notes: Only fix was regenerating missing ~/.ssh/id_ed25519.pub, which the report itself acknowledges does not affect SSH authentication. Did not identify or fix any issue related to the actual connection failures. Declared SSH config 'correct' despite the IP-matching gap.

output_a.notes: Reports key permission 600 and directory permission 700.

output_b.notes: Reports key permission 600, directory permission 700, and owner dev:dev.

criteria.description: Check known_hosts and SSH config configuration

output_a.notes: Reports known_hosts hashed format, cached vs newly added keys. Shows SSH config before/after modification. Identifies config gap with IP-based connections.

output_b.notes: Reports known_hosts with no stale entries. Shows SSH config. Additionally checks remote authorized_keys on all 3 nodes and SSHD service status. However, missed the IP-matching config gap.

criteria.description: Diagnose aether configuration and cluster connection status

output_a.notes: Full aether CLI version, config validation, Nomad/Consul versions, all 8 node statuses, 3 server nodes with leader identification.

output_b.notes: Shows aether config YAML, Nomad/Consul versions, all 8 nodes, server info, Consul 11-member details.

output_a.content_rationale: Excellent diagnostic coverage. Key differentiator is identifying a real, actionable SSH config issue (Host pattern not matching IP addresses) that plausibly explains the original failure. Fix is well-reasoned with before/after diff. Root cause analysis is thorough. Also includes secondary findings (locale warnings). Covers all diagnostic dimensions requested.
output_a.structure_rationale: Clean, well-organized with consistent formatting. Status badges ([OK], [FIXED]) provide immediate visual summary. Tables are uniform. Before/after code blocks for the fix are clear. Diagnostic summary section at the end provides quick overview. Professional diagnostic report format throughout.

output_b.content_rationale: Good diagnostic coverage with unique strengths (remote auth logs, authorized_keys verification, SSHD status/uptime checks). However, the only applied fix (regenerating .pub file) is self-admittedly unrelated to the reported problem. Critically, it declares SSH config 'correct' while missing the IP-matching gap that Output A identified. Possible causes section is speculative without acting on any of them.
output_b.structure_rationale: Good structure with numbered sections, tables, and clear headers. Well-organized separation of test results, actions taken, and recommendations. Slightly less polished than Output A -- lacks status badges for quick scanning. The language inconsistency (English report for Chinese eval) is minor.

blind_comparison.winner_rationale: Output A wins decisively on the most critical criterion: identifying and fixing the actual problem. It found a genuine SSH config gap (Host patterns not matching IP addresses) that directly explains why connections via IP could fail while hostname-based connections succeed. Output A applied a meaningful fix and provided clear before/after documentation. Output B performed good remote-side diagnostics but missed this config issue entirely, declaring the config 'correct'. Its only fix (regenerating .pub) was cosmetic. Output A also has superior report structure with status badges and a clean summary.
